@graph.headline: Navigating SDIRA Crypto Compliance: A Sage's Guide to Secure Retirement Investing
@graph.description: Learn how to safely invest in cryptocurrency within your Self-Directed IRA (SDIRA). Our expert guide covers custodians, prohibited transactions, and tax compliance.

mainEntity.name: What is the most important step for SDIRA crypto compliance?

acceptedAnswer.text: The most critical step is partnering with a specialized qualified custodian. They ensure your assets are held legally, compliant with IRS guidelines, and that all reporting is accurate, which protects your retirement account's tax-advantaged status.

mainEntity.name: Can I hold my retirement crypto in my own personal digital wallet?

acceptedAnswer.text: No. Holding the private keys to your retirement crypto assets in a personal wallet is considered a prohibited transaction under IRS rules and can result in the disqualification of your SDIRA.

mainEntity.name: What are UBTI and UDFI in the context of SDIRA investing?

acceptedAnswer.text: UBTI (Unrelated Business Taxable Income) and UDFI (Unrelated Debt-Financed Income) are taxes that can apply to retirement accounts if they engage in certain active business activities or use debt financing for investments. You should consult a tax professional before engaging in staking or yield-generating activities.
